mechanism_narrative: Sustained momentum in nonfarm payrolls growth indicates a robust economic expansion, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2009 PAYEMS shows a persistent upward trend
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 6-month changes in PAYEMS and 60-day forward returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from its recent low signals the onset of a recession and a cyclical peak in the labor market, causing a flight to safety and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:The 'Sahm Rule' uses a similar mechanism as a recession indicator
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of instances within 60 trading days following a trigger event over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is raising interest rates while industrial production growth is decelerating, profit margins are squeezed and discount rates rise simultaneously, leading to sharply negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed began a tightening cycle in Dec 2015
failure_condition: The combined signal does not produce a statistically significant negative return distribution (t-test p > 0.05) compared to non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment, accelerating inflation is interpreted by markets as a sign of strong aggregate demand and corporate pricing power, boosting earnings expectations and leading to positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC low interest rate environment persists
failure_condition: The correlation between CPIAUCSL 3-month changes and S&P 500 forward returns turns negative and stays negative for over 2 years while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in retail sales growth, measured as a deviation from its recent trend, signals robust consumer demand, which translates to higher expected corporate revenues and positive short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Monthly % change in RSAFS > 1.5 standard deviations above its 12-month average
failure_condition: The average 5-day return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained deceleration in industrial production growth indicates weakening manufacturing and business investment, leading to downward revisions in earnings forecasts and negative equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of INDPRO YoY growth falls below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key component of the Conference Board Coincident Economic Index
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate (correctly predicting negative 60-day returns) is below 50% over any 4-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals a contraction in a key cyclical sector, indicating falling consumer confidence and tighter credit conditions, which negatively impacts broader equity market returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST monthly value falls > 10% below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a major leading indicator of the business cycle
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return following a trigger is non-negative over a full business cycle (e.g., 2010-2017).

mechanism_narrative: When nominal growth is driven more by inflation than by real output, it signals deteriorating economic quality. If CPI growth outpaces real GDP growth, this implies margin compression for firms and leads to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Quarterly YoY % change in CPIAUCSL > Quarterly YoY % change in GDPC1
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger is active fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a 5-year period.
